deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: i m getting really bothered that i m saying the same thing over and over again and it s fascinating to me that my english speakers are not understanding me

scenes.rationale: The teacher characterizes students as a group ('my english speakers') by their failure to understand, locating the problem in the students rather than the instruction, and frames it as surprising/deficient that they aren't grasping it.

deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: why is student b the only one here that knows what to do

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: draw a picture to show how we solved it the same way we did two times over here student b what do you want to add if you re confused go see student b s any questions right now wonderful get to work

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: which one are you missing tell me in english nice job where is the black marker i was using it wasn t i i will look for your black marker and when i find it i will give it to you beautiful write all that here then draw me a picture maybe an array side conversation why is student b the only one here that knows what to do

context_excerpt.speaker: student
context_excerpt.text: student i student e they did this

scenes.rationale: Defines the whole class by what they lack—implying that everyone except one student does not know what to do—a comparative/sweeping deficit characterization.

deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: he was the only one who knew what to do and you were a goofball on camera

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: oranges can you write that whole sentence i bet that only help him when he needs help don t show him the answer can you write that whole sentence beautiful i m sorry he was the only one who knew what to do and you were a goofball on camera i m not going to give you candy for that side conversation boys and girls unfortunately our time is up our kindergarten buddies are coming in a minute if you have one or two quick things to add to your poster please do that i d like you to gather back on the rug if you haven t had time to finish your poster we can finish them tomorrow thank you very much

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: interesting you guys did division here let s take a look when ms s orders oranges from the fruit company they come in boxes each box has 40 oranges inside last week ms s ordered 6 boxes

scenes.rationale: Labels a student ('goofball') and frames the rest comparatively as not knowing what to do, attributing failure to the student rather than the task.

deficit_span.speaker: teacher
deficit_span.verbatim_quote: well since you haven t done much today i don t think you really have a right to be tired

context_excerpt.text: can we have snack time right now

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: can you stop touching that again please thank you we re going to go back to our seats we re going to do a little bit of independent work in our books i m going to show you the page page 55 okay

context_excerpt.speaker: student
context_excerpt.text: i m tired of doing work

context_excerpt.speaker: teacher
context_excerpt.text: well since you haven t done much today i don t think you really have a right to be tired she s telling you not to touch she doesn t want you to get in trouble she s a good friend thank you stop it

scenes.rationale: Attributes the student's stated fatigue to a lack of effort/productivity, a deficit framing of the student's motivation rather than addressing the work.
